sections.paragraph:
ἄρρητος, ον (cp. ῥῆμα; Hom.+; Sb 7600, 7; TestSol 8:7 D; JosAs 12:5 cod. A 27:2; GrBar ins 1; ApcZeph; Philo; Jos., Bell. 7, 262; Just., D. 127, 2 al.; Ath. 6, 1; Tat. 34, 3; for spelling B-D-F §11, 1)

① of someth. that cannot be expressed, since it is beyond human powers, inexpressible (Pla., Symp. 189b; Plut., Mor. 564f; Herm. Wr. 1, 31; PGM 13, 763 explains it: ἐν ἀνθρώπου στόματι λαληθῆναι οὐ δύναται).

② of someth. that must not be expressed, since it is holy, not to be spoken (since Eur., Bacch. 472; Hdt. 5, 83, 3; Thessalus [I a.d.] adjures Asclepius διʼ ἀρρήτων ὀνομάτων: Cat. Cod. Astr. VIII/3, 137. Not infreq. on sacral ins [Nägeli 55]. PGM 3, 205; 12, 237; Vett. Val. 19, 1; Plut., Mor. 360f; Mesomedes in: Alex. Coll. p. 198, no. 36, 14=IAndrosIsis, Mesomedes p. 145 ln. 14; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 175) ἄ. ῥήματα words too sacred to tell 2 Cor 12:4 (cp. Lucian, Epigr. 11).—DELG s.v. 2 εἴρω. M-M. TW.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

ApcZeph ApcZeph = Apocalypse of Zephaniah (Sophonia)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

someth. someth. = something

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Mesomedes Mesomedes = Isis hymn of Mesomedes

IAndrosIsis IAndrosIsis = Der Isishymnus von Andros und verwandte Texte—List 3

  1. ln. = line

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 134
